WorkoutDescription:
AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 8km | D+: 50m | Duration: 50min
Flat route along Arve or lakeside. HR strictly under 138. Conversational pace. Black coffee only before.

LUNCH SESSION — STRENGTH A (50min)
Eccentric squats, step-ups, core. See Strength A program.
WARM-UP (5min): Leg swings x15 each, glute bridges x15, bodyweight squats x15
MAIN: Back Squat eccentric 4x6 (4s down) — Bulgarian Split Squat 3x8 each — Weighted Step-ups 3x10 each (40cm box) — Nordic Curls 3x6 — Calf Raises eccentric 3x12
CORE: Plank 45s → Dead Bug 10 each → Pallof Press 10 each
COOL-DOWN: Hip flexor stretch 60s each, pigeon 60s each

AM SESSION — HILL REPEATS
Distance: 9km | D+: 300m | Duration: 65min
10min easy warm-up on flat (Z1). Main set: 6x4min on moderate incline (10-15% grade), effort at Z3 (HR 153-168). Walk/jog down recovery between reps (HR must drop below 140). 10min easy cool-down. Focus on short steps, upright posture, active arms.

LUNCH SESSION — MOBILITY (20min)
Hip openers: 90/90 stretch 60s each, pigeon pose 60s each
Ankle mobility: wall ankle stretch 45s each
Foam roll: quads 2min, calves 2min, IT band 2min

AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 7km | D+: 50m | Duration: 45min
Flat route. HR under 153. Easy effort, recovery from yesterday's hill repeats.

LUNCH SESSION — STRENGTH B (45min)
Upper body pole-specific + core. See Strength B program.
MAIN: Lat Pulldown 3x10 — Tricep Dips 3x12 — Shoulder Press 3x10 — Bent-over Rows 3x10
CORE: Russian Twists 3x15 — Side Planks 3x30s each — Mountain Climbers 3x20
COOL-DOWN: Thoracic opener, lat stretch, pec stretch

AM SESSION — SALÈVE CLIMB
Distance: 10km | D+: 800m | D-: 800m | Duration: 2h15
Route: Pas de l'Échelle → summit via main trail.
ASCENT (~1h30): Hike with poles at race effort. Target vertical speed: 400-450 m/h. HR in Z2 (138-153). Short steps, upright posture, rhythmic pole plants. This is your first mountain session — find your climbing rhythm.
DESCENT (~45min): Easy controlled run down. Short quick steps on technical sections. No hard braking. Practice pole stowage for runnable descents.

No lunch session — single big AM session.

REST DAY
Light 20min mobility/stretching only. No running, no strength.
Hip flexors, hamstrings, calves, thoracic spine. Foam roll if needed.
10,000 steps walking throughout the day (NEAT for weight cut).
Morning weigh-in. Hydrate 3L+. LOW calorie day.

Details: 22km / 900m D+ — Jura or Salève traverse, poles
WorkoutDescription:
KEY SESSION — LONG MOUNTAIN RUN
Distance: 22km | D+: 900m | D-: 900m | Duration: 3h-3h30
Route: Jura traverse (e.g., La Faucille → Col de la Faucille loop) or Salève south traverse.
Wear poles. Practice race fueling: target 80g carbs/hour (C90 sachet every hour + sips of water). Start eating from minute 30.
Pace: Walk all climbs >15% with poles. Run flats at 8:30-9:00/km. Easy controlled descents.
HR: Stay in Z1-Z2 entire run. If HR climbs above 153 on climbs, slow down.
Post-run: Large recovery meal within 30min. HIGH calorie day.

This is your first long mountain run — focus on rhythm and fueling, not speed.

EASY RECOVERY RUN
Distance: 6km | D+: 30m | Duration: 40min
Flat route only. HR strictly under 138. This is active recovery after yesterday's long run.
Shake out the legs. If anything is sore, walk instead.
Morning weigh-in (official Monday weigh-in tomorrow).
LOW calorie day — high protein, minimal carbs.

AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 9km | D+: 50m | Duration: 55min
Flat, easy. HR under 138. Monday weigh-in before breakfast.

LUNCH SESSION — STRENGTH A (55min)
Heavy eccentric focus this week.
MAIN: Back Squat eccentric 5x5 (4s down, increase weight from last week) — Weighted Step-ups 3x10 each (40cm box) — Nordic Curls 3x6 — Bulgarian Split Squats 3x8 each — Calf Raises eccentric 3x12
CORE: Plank 45s → Dead Bug 10 each → Pallof Press 10 each
MODERATE calorie day.

AM SESSION — SALÈVE REPEATS
Distance: 14km | D+: 1,200m | D-: 1,200m | Duration: 2h30
Route: Monnetier or Pas de l'Échelle start.
REP 1: Hike up with poles (~600m D+, ~45min ascent). Target vertical speed 450 m/h. HR in Z2. Controlled run down (~20min descent).
Rest 5-10min at bottom. Refuel with gel.
REP 2: Same route, same effort. Focus on maintaining vertical speed even though tired.
Controlled run down. Cool-down walk 5min.

LUNCH SESSION — MOBILITY (20min)
Hip openers, ankle mobility, foam roll quads/calves.
HIGH calorie day — you earned it.

AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 8km | D+: 50m | Duration: 50min
Flat, easy. Recovery from yesterday's Salève repeats.

LUNCH SESSION — STRENGTH B (45min)
Lat Pulldown 3x10 — Tricep Dips 3x12 — Shoulder Press 3x10 — Bent-over Rows 3x10
CORE: Russian Twists 3x15 — Side Planks 3x30s each — Mountain Climbers 3x20
MODERATE calorie day.

AM SESSION — TRAIL TEMPO
Distance: 11km | D+: 200m | Duration: 70min
15min easy warm-up (Z1). Main set: 3x10min at Z2-Z3 (HR 138-168) on rolling trail terrain. 5min easy jog recovery between reps. 10min cool-down.
Focus on maintaining consistent effort on varied terrain — don't surge on uphills, don't coast on downhills. Smooth, controlled rhythm.
MODERATE calorie day.

REST DAY
Light 15min mobility only. No running, no strength.
10,000 steps walking. Morning weigh-in. Hydrate 3L+.
LOW calorie day — high protein, minimal carbs. No starch.

Details: 28km / 1,500m D+ — mountain long run, poles, full nutrition practice
WorkoutDescription:
KEY SESSION — LONG MOUNTAIN RUN
Distance: 28km | D+: 1,500m | D-: 1,500m | Duration: 4h
Route: Jura traverse or Salève extended circuit.
Poles required. Full race nutrition practice: C90 sachet every hour (80-90g carbs/h), water 500ml/h.
Walk ALL climbs with poles. Target vertical speed: 450 m/h early, accept 400 m/h late.
Run flats at 8:30-9:00/km. Controlled descents.
HR: Z1-Z2 entire run. Discipline on climbs — if HR goes above 153, slow down.
Bring full hydration vest. Practice aid station routine at halfway point (refill, eat something salty, 3min max stop).
Post-run: Large recovery meal. HIGH calorie day.

This is a significant step up from last week (22km → 28km, 900m → 1500m D+). Pace conservatively.

Details: 10km / 600m D+ — back-to-back mountain hike with poles
WorkoutDescription:
BACK-TO-BACK SESSION
Distance: 10km | D+: 600m | D-: 600m | Duration: 1h30
Easy mountain hike with poles. This is NOT a run — hike up, walk/easy jog down.
Purpose: Train on tired legs from yesterday's long run. This simulates hours 8-14 of the race.
HR under 138. If legs are heavy, walk the entire thing.
Practice eating while hiking — bring a gel and bar, eat on the move.
HIGH calorie day (back-to-back weekend gets full fuel).

AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 8km | D+: 50m | Duration: 50min
Flat, easy. Monday weigh-in. Recovery from weekend back-to-back.

LUNCH SESSION — STRENGTH A (55min)
Eccentric focus — this is the heaviest strength week.
MAIN: Back Squat eccentric 4x6 (4s down, heavy) — Bulgarian Split Squats 3x8 each — Nordic Curls 3x6 — Weighted Step-ups 3x10 each — Calf Raises eccentric 3x12
CORE: Plank 45s → Dead Bug 10 each → Pallof Press 10 each
MODERATE calorie day.

AM SESSION — STEEP HILL REPEATS
Distance: 10km | D+: 500m | Duration: 75min
10min easy warm-up. Main set: 8x3min on STEEP incline (>15% grade). Hard hiking effort, Z3-Z4 (HR 153-183). Walk down recovery (HR below 140 before next rep). 10min cool-down jog.
Focus: Power hiking at high intensity — arms driving, poles planted aggressively, short powerful steps.

LUNCH SESSION — MOBILITY (25min)
Extended mobility: hip openers, ankle mobility, thoracic rotation, foam roll all lower body.
HIGH calorie day.

AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 7km | D+: 50m | Duration: 45min
Flat, easy. Recovery day between hard sessions.

LUNCH SESSION — STRENGTH B (40min)
Pole-specific upper body + core circuit.
Lat Pulldown 3x10 — Tricep Dips 3x12 — Shoulder Press 3x10 — Bent-over Rows 3x10
CORE: Russian Twists 3x15 — Side Planks 3x30s each
MODERATE calorie day.

Details: 16km / 1,000m D+ — Salève full climb + traverse, poles
WorkoutDescription:
AM SESSION — MOUNTAIN-SPECIFIC
Distance: 16km | D+: 1,000m | D-: 1,000m | Duration: 2h30
Route: Salève full climb from Pas de l'Échelle + ridge traverse to Pitons or beyond.
Hike up with poles. Target vertical speed: 450 m/h (this is your race target — lock it in). Practice walking rhythm: breathe in for 3 steps, out for 3 steps.
Run the traverse and descent at easy pace.
HR in Z2 (138-153) on climbs. If above 153, you're going too hard.
No lunch session — single big AM session.
HIGH calorie day.
Focus: Sustained climbing at race vertical speed

WorkoutDescription:
REST DAY
Light 15min mobility only. Critical rest before the biggest weekend of the plan.
Prep gear for tomorrow's 5 AM start: vest, poles, nutrition, headlamp, full race kit.
LOW calorie day.
Bed by 9:00 PM — wake at 4:30 AM tomorrow.

Details: 37km / 2,000m D+ — KEY SESSION, 5:00 AM start, full race kit + nutrition
WorkoutDescription:
*** KEY SESSION OF THE PLAN ***
Distance: 37km | D+: 2,000m | D-: 2,000m | Duration: 5h-5h30
START: 5:00 AM (practice race-day wake-up routine)

Route: Jura extended traverse or Salève + Voirons combo. Choose terrain with sustained climbs of 500m+ and technical descents.

GEAR: Full race kit — vest, poles, headlamp (it's dark at 5 AM), all nutrition you'd carry in the race.

NUTRITION: Full race protocol — C90 every hour (80-90g carbs/h), water 500-700ml/h, bar every 2 hours. Salt cap every 45-60min. Start eating at minute 30. Practice your aid station routine at km 18 (set a bag with refills).

PACING:
- Walk ALL climbs with poles. Vertical speed: 450 m/h target
- Run flats: 8:30-9:00/km
- Descents: controlled, quick steps, 7:00-8:00/km easy terrain
- HR: NEVER above 153. This is not a race — it's a rehearsal

MENTAL: Log how you feel at hour 3, hour 4, hour 5. Note what works and what doesn't. This is your dress rehearsal — adjust everything for race day based on today.

Post-run: Immediate large meal. Foam roll. Compression. HIGH+ calorie day — eat freely.
Focus: Race simulation — pacing, fueling, kit, early start

Details: 13km / 700m D+ — back-to-back mountain, easy, poles, eat while hiking
WorkoutDescription:
BACK-TO-BACK SESSION
Distance: 13km | D+: 700m | D-: 700m | Duration: 2h
Easy mountain session on tired legs. Hike up with poles, walk or easy jog down.
HR under 138. This should feel like hours 10-12 of the race — heavy legs, tired, but still moving.
Practice eating while hiking. Bring 2 gels and a bar. Eat everything.
If legs are destroyed from yesterday: reduce to 1h30 / 10km / 500m D+. Listen to your body.
HIGH calorie day.

AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 7km | D+: 50m | Duration: 45min
Flat, easy. Monday weigh-in. Recover from peak weekend.

LUNCH SESSION — STRENGTH A (40min)
Maintenance only — lighter than previous weeks.
MAIN: Back Squat eccentric 3x6 (moderate weight) — Step-ups 2x8 each — Nordic Curls 2x6
CORE: Plank 45s → Dead Bug 10 each → Pallof Press 10 each
MODERATE calorie day.

AM SESSION — MOUNTAIN TRAIL TEMPO
Distance: 12km | D+: 400m | Duration: 75min
15min easy warm-up on trail. Main set: 4x8min at Z2-Z3 (HR 138-168) on undulating mountain trail. Include uphill and downhill sections in each rep. 4min easy jog between reps. 10min cool-down.
Focus: Race-pace effort on race-like terrain. Practice smooth gear changes between climb, flat, descent.

LUNCH SESSION — MOBILITY (20min)
Hips, ankles, thoracic. Foam roll.
MODERATE calorie day.

AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 6km | D+: 30m | Duration: 40min
Flat, easy.

LUNCH SESSION — STRENGTH B (30min)
Light upper body + core only. Last strength session of the plan.
Lat Pulldown 2x10 — Shoulder Press 2x10 — Bent-over Rows 2x10
CORE: Plank 30s — Side Plank 20s each — Dead Bug 8 each
MODERATE calorie day.

REST DAY
Light 20min mobility only. Saving legs for Saturday dress rehearsal.
Prep ALL race gear for Saturday: vest, poles, nutrition (pack exactly what you'll carry race day), headlamp, layers, mandatory gear checklist.
LOW calorie day.
Bed by 9:00 PM.

SHAKEOUT + STRIDES
Distance: 5km | D+: 20m | Duration: 30min
20min very easy jog (Z1). Then 4x20s strides at fast but controlled pace (not sprinting), with 60s walk between.
Purpose: Activate legs for tomorrow's dress rehearsal without fatiguing them.
MODERATE calorie day. Eat well — tomorrow is a big day.

Details: 40km / 2,300m D+ — DRESS REHEARSAL, 5:00 AM start, full race kit + nutrition + poles
WorkoutDescription:
*** DRESS REHEARSAL — RACE SIMULATION ***
Distance: 40km | D+: 2,300m | D-: 2,300m | Duration: 5h30-6h
START: 5:00 AM — identical to race day

PRE-RUN: Wake at 3:00 AM. Pre-race meal at 3:15 (2 rice cakes + honey + half banana + coffee). Same as race day plan.

ROUTE: Maximize mountain terrain. Ideally include one climb of 800m+ continuous. Choose terrain similar to Aravis if possible.

GEAR: EVERYTHING you'll wear/carry on race day. Exact same vest, shoes, socks, poles, layers, anti-chafe. Test it all today.

NUTRITION: Exact race protocol. C90 every hour, bar every 2h, salt caps every 45min, water 500-700ml/h. Set up a 'mock aid station' at km 20 with a drop bag.

PACING:
- Walk ALL climbs: 450 m/h early, accept 400 m/h after 3h
- Flats: 8:30-9:00/km
- Descents: controlled, 7:00-8:30/km
- HR: Max 153. Race discipline.

AID STATION PRACTICE: At your mock aid station (km 20), practice the full routine: refill water, eat salty food, swap anything needed, leave in <5 min.

LOG EVERYTHING: How did nutrition sit? Any chafing? Shoe comfort? Pole strap comfort? Vest bounce? What would you change for race day?

Post-run: Immediate large meal. Full recovery protocol. HIGH+ calorie day.
Focus: Complete race simulation — final test of everything

WorkoutDescription:
EASY RECOVERY RUN
Distance: 7km | D+: 30m | Duration: 45min
Flat, very easy. HR under 138. Shake out the legs from yesterday's dress rehearsal.
Note any residual soreness or issues — these need addressing before race day.
MODERATE calorie day.

Details: 6km / 30m D+ — flat, fasted. STOP CREATINE TODAY
WorkoutDescription:
AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 6km | D+: 30m | Duration: 40min
Flat, easy. Monday weigh-in.
*** STOP CREATINE TODAY — last dose was yesterday. Expect 1-2kg water weight drop over next 10 days. ***

LUNCH SESSION — LIGHT STRENGTH + MOBILITY (30min)
Bodyweight squats 2x15, glute bridges 2x12, planks 2x30s, then 15min mobility.
This is the last strength session. Keep it light.
MODERATE calorie day.

AM SESSION — HILL REPEATS (final quality session)
Distance: 8km | D+: 300m | Duration: 50min
10min easy warm-up. Main set: 5x3min at Z3 (HR 153-168) on moderate incline. Walk/jog recovery. 10min cool-down.
This is your LAST hard session. Enjoy it. Run with confidence.

LUNCH SESSION — MOBILITY (20min)
Full lower body mobility + foam roll.
MODERATE calorie day.

EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 5km | D+: 20m | Duration: 35min
Flat, very easy. HR under 138.
No lunch session.
LOW calorie day.

MOUNTAIN RUN
Distance: 10km | D+: 700m | D-: 700m | Duration: 1h30
Salève or local mountain. Hike up with poles, easy run down.
HR in Z1-Z2. Enjoy the mountain — this is one of your last mountain sessions before race day.
No lunch session.
HIGH calorie day.

REST DAY
Light 15min mobility only.
10,000 steps walking.
LOW calorie day.
Prep nutrition for tomorrow's last long run — test your final race-day nutrition setup.

Details: 20km / 1,100m D+ — LAST LONG RUN, test final race nutrition
WorkoutDescription:
LAST LONG RUN
Distance: 20km | D+: 1,100m | D-: 1,100m | Duration: 2h30-3h
Mountain route. Poles.
This is your FINAL long run before the race. Purpose: confirm nutrition plan works, enjoy the mountains, build confidence.
Nutrition: Use exact race-day products and timing. Final test — no changes after today.
Pacing: Z1-Z2. Don't push. You should finish feeling like you could do another 2 hours.
Post-run: Recovery meal. Foam roll. Note any final adjustments for race day.
HIGH calorie day.

EASY RUN
Distance: 5km | D+: 20m | Duration: 30min
Flat, very easy. Active recovery.
LOW calorie day.

AM SESSION — EASY RUN (fasted)
Distance: 5km | D+: 20m | Duration: 30min
Flat, easy. Monday weigh-in.
You may feel restless with reduced volume — this is normal. Trust the taper.

LUNCH — LIGHT MOBILITY (20min)
Gentle stretching and foam roll. No strength work from here.
MODERATE calorie day.

SHORT QUALITY SESSION
Distance: 6km | D+: 50m | Duration: 35min
15min easy warm-up. 4x2min at Z3 (HR 153-168) with 2min easy jog between. 10min cool-down.
Short, sharp. Keep the engine tuned. You should feel snappy.
LOW calorie day.

AM SESSION — EASY RUN
Distance: 4km | D+: 10m | Duration: 25min
Flat, minimal. Just moving.

LUNCH — LIGHT CORE (15min)
Planks, dead bugs, glute bridges. Very light. Keep core activated.
LOW calorie day.

EASY HIKE WITH POLES
Distance: 5km | D+: 350m | D-: 350m | Duration: 45min
Gentle mountain walk with poles. Last time touching the mountain before race day.
HR under 153. Enjoy it. Visualize race day on the climbs.
MODERATE calorie day.

REST DAY
Light 15min mobility. Gentle stretching.
LOW calorie day.
Pack race bag if not already done. Check mandatory gear list.

SHAKEOUT + STRIDES
Distance: 5km | D+: 20m | Duration: 30min
25min easy jog, then 3x15s strides at fast comfortable pace (walk 60s between).
Legs should feel light and ready. If they feel heavy, don't worry — taper heaviness is normal and clears by race day.
MODERATE calorie day.

Details: 3km / 10m D+ — flat, minimal, or complete rest
WorkoutDescription:
EASY JOG OR REST
Distance: 3km | D+: 10m | Duration: 20min
Very easy jog or complete rest — your choice. Listen to your body.
If you feel good, jog. If you feel tired, rest. Both are fine.
LOW calorie day.

EASY JOG
Distance: 3km | D+: 10m | Duration: 20min
Last run before the shakeout on Wednesday. Very easy.
Normal eating, well-hydrated.
MODERATE calorie day.

LIGHT MOBILITY + ACTIVATION
20min of: glute activation drills (clamshells, banded walks, glute bridges), gentle hip openers, ankle mobility.
No running. No strength.
Normal eating. Stay hydrated.
MODERATE calorie day.

FINAL SHAKEOUT
Distance: 2km | D+: 0m | Duration: 15min
10min very easy jog + 3x15s strides (walk 60s between). That's it.
Legs should feel sharp and ready.

BEGIN CARB LOADING: 6-7g carbs/kg = 570-665g carbs today.
Switch to white rice, pasta, bread, pancakes, honey, jam, fruit juice. Reduce fiber.

Details: FULL REST — J-2 heavy carb loading
WorkoutDescription:
FULL REST — NO EXERCISE

HEAVY CARB LOADING: 8-10g/kg = 760-950g carbs today.
Eat every 2-3 hours: white rice, white pasta, white bread, pancakes, honey, jam, fruit juice, sports drinks.
Minimal fiber and fat. You will feel bloated — this is normal.
Expect +1-2kg water weight — this is glycogen fuel, not fat.

Stay hydrated: 3L+ water. Light walking only.
Bed by 9:30 PM.

Details: FULL REST — J-1, pack race bag, early bed
WorkoutDescription:
FULL REST — NO EXERCISE

Big carb breakfast + big pasta lunch.
EARLY LIGHT DINNER by 6:00 PM: white rice + simple sauce. Nothing heavy.

RACE PREP:
- Pack race bag (see Gear Checklist)
- Lay out race clothes
- Pin bib
- Charge headlamp + watch
- Set 2 alarms for 3:00 AM
- Prep pre-race meal (rice cakes, honey, banana, coffee)

Bed by 9:00 PM. Room dark and cool (18°C).
Magnesium glycinate at 8:30 PM.

You are ready. Trust the work you've done.

Details: 76.9km / 5,000m D+ — Trail du Gypaète Les 3 Lacs — SUB-14H GOAL
WorkoutDescription:
*** RACE DAY — TRAIL DU GYPAÈTE LES 3 LACS ***
Distance: 76.9km | D+: 5,000m | D-: 5,000m | Goal: Sub-14 hours

3:00 AM — Wake up, drink 300ml water
3:15 AM — Pre-race meal: 2 rice cakes + honey + half banana + coffee
4:00 AM — Stop eating, sip water only
5:00 AM — START

PACING STRATEGY:
- Flat (<5%): 8:00-9:00/km, Z1-Z2
- Moderate climb (5-15%): 12-15/km, Z2, power hike with poles
- Steep climb (>15%): 18-25/km, Z2-Z3, hike with poles
- Easy descent: 7:00-8:00/km
- Technical descent: 9:00-12:00/km

FUELING: 80-90g carbs/hour from hour 1. C90 every hour, bar every 2h, salt cap every 45min, water 500-700ml/h. Caffeine 100mg at hour 3, then every 3h.

GOLDEN RULES:
1. Walk ALL climbs
2. Never exceed Z3 on climbs (HR <168)
3. Start conservative — first 3h should feel easy
4. Run flats and easy descents — this is where you make time
5. Short quick steps on descents
6. Aid stations: under 5 minutes
7. DO NOT SIT DOWN

You trained for this. You did the work. Execute the plan.

Details: Post-race recovery — no running for 7-10 days
WorkoutDescription:
POST-RACE RECOVERY
No running. Walk only if you can. Foam roll gently. Eat well — your body needs fuel to repair.
Compression socks. Elevate legs.
Celebrate — you just finished a 77K mountain ultra.

Recovery timeline:
- Days 1-7: No running. Walking, swimming, gentle bike only
- Days 8-14: Light 30-40min easy runs, 3x/week
- Week 3+: Resume normal easy running
- Week 4+: Begin 114K Nice build phase
